steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The nitrogen-treated SiC fiber exhibited a lower minimum creep rate than the polymer-derived fibers. A lower creep rate indicates the material deforms more slowly under stress—the key signal that reveals which fiber is more effective at resisting creep.

steps.title: Connect to the choices
steps.content:
A fiber that resists creep better will allow composites to hold up longer under stress—so the claim that nitrogen-treated composites can withstand mechanical stress for longer than polymer-derived ones directly follows from the finding.

The other three options either tack on an awkward condition or wrongly suggest the polymer-derived fibers are superior.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
